title: Across Cultures 1
description: The British Isles – Kultur, Essen, Dialekte

vocab.ex: Let's form a music group.
The UK is a country. Bavaria and California are states.
Combine all these words and you can make a sentence.
A mouse looks tiny next to a horse.
In the photo the queen is wearing a crown.
The Channel Islands are crown dependencies, just like the Isle of Man.
Henry VIII and Elizabeth I were two famous monarchs from the Tudor family.
When you've opened a bottle of juice, you should finish it within a few days.
If you want to travel from Germany to the UK, you'll need a passport.
A lion is a large animal of the cat family.
In sport, the shirt you play in is a jersey.
I wonder what Tom's party theme will be. Have a guess!
Did you hear that Tom and Emma are together? What's so surprising about that?

vocab.de: Dialekt und Einwohner der Umgebung von Newcastle upon Tyne
Was ist los?; Was geht ab?

vocab.ex: Give me something to drink, please. I have a bad taste in my mouth!
If you don't eat meat, then Irish stew isn't the dish for you.
Welsh people are from Wales. Some of them speak Welsh.
Yorkshire pudding is an example of how puddings aren't always sweet.
St Ives is a famous town on the northern Cornish coast.
A traditional pasty had meat in one end and an apple in the other.
I love my scones with a special jam from France.
Some people put the clotted cream on top of the jam, others put the jam on the cream.
Welsh rarebit is a quick and easy snack.
Haggis is a Scottish speciality.
Andy, do you have any idea how many calories are in that piece of cake?
The price for the tickets is £5 per person.
I don't like this restaurant, the servings are too small.
You can argue about things here in class, but stay polite.
Butter is a milk product.
You need baking powder in the recipes for many kinds of cakes.
Most restaurants have salt and pepper on each table.
Carrot pudding is a popular dessert from India.
One burger with no onions, please.
Potatoes came from South America to Europe.
For religious reasons, you won't find beef on menus in most parts of India.
You usually only see the word currant in baking recipes.
Raisins and currants are almost the same thing, but currants are smaller.
What kind of dishes do you like best: sweet or savoury?
